matched_projects.project_title: Workflow File Variable & Update Workspace Info Automation

tailored_bullets.text: Led T1 technical scoping and web/platform interface definition for file-type workspace variables and their use in Update Workspace Info automation within a workflow platform.

Implemented web-side support for FILE workspace variables across template builder, flow execution, and runtime workspace editing, including temp-board upload flow, payload persistence, and DDR exposure.
Designed and delivered file-variable support in Update Workspace Info automation using separate workspace_file_variables modeling and attachment-to-variable mapping via customData.variableId.
Built and refined add/edit/submit/load flows for automation file variables, including validation, preview/runtime display, invalid DDR handling, and post-execution attachment reconstruction.

matched_projects.project_title: MV-17930 Scheduled Flow Preview Flow Member List Tuning

tailored_bullets.text: Led web-side technical analysis and implementation for MV-17930, a workflow/calendar feature that corrected assignee and viewer display in scheduled flow preview and calendar detail views.

Defined and implemented a role-state persistence approach using workflow variable custom_data.is_viewer_role to distinguish assigned vs. unassigned template roles during scheduled flow create/edit flows.
Used role actNumber to classify assigned and unassigned roles at request-build time, improving consistency between scheduling configuration and downstream UI rendering.
Updated request-payload and Vue rendering logic across scheduling flow modules and calendar member-list computation to correctly display assignees, viewers, and deduplicated participants.

matched_projects.project_title: MV-18768 Form Enhancement P4 - UX Tuning

tailored_bullets.text: Delivered Web-side UX enhancements for Form Builder and Form Template Builder, improving image upload interactions and show/hide rule field clarity.
Owned technical scope analysis and frontend design breakdown for a PM-scoped UX tuning task, translating approved requirements into a structured Vue implementation.
Designed a local-cache-plus-deferred-upload image flow using blob/base64 conversion and thumbnail URLs to provide instant visual feedback during editing.
Implemented immediate thumbnail synchronization between form canvas and right-panel editor without waiting for backend resource persistence.

matched_projects.project_title: MNF-248 contactsStore lifecycle cleanup across user selectors

tailored_bullets.text: Resolved cross-UI state interference in a shared Vue/Pinia contactsStore used by multiple contact-selection workflows in Moxo New Flow.
Implemented lifecycle cleanup with destroyStore() in shared selector components to prevent stale contact state from leaking across Workspace List and in-workspace flows.
Redesigned ContactListFilterSelect.vue to preserve selected-label display through local caching, keeping UI behavior stable after shared store reset.
Supported regression-safe frontend delivery across Workspace List filtering, Automation Send Email, DocuSign signer selection, coordinator invitation, and member reassignment flows.
Implemented my T1 work included technical scope analysis, skill/platform assessment, implementation estimation, and defining behavior/interface expectations across platforms and layers
Implemented i translated business scenarios into technical rules, exception handling, and engineering-ready definitions
Implemented :contentReference[oaicite:33]{index=33}

Implemented my T2 work focused on web development and detailed implementation design, including role data type updates, workflow role conversion, controller/template default-user loading, store validation, participant form behavior, selector updates, and UI rendering for default assignees and unassigned roles
Led web technical analysis and detailed implementation design for a cross-platform Shared Workspaces UX update in contact profiles, aligning behavior across Web, iOS, Android, ISDK, and Biz.
Translated PM-defined product changes into a structured Vue/web solution with collapsed-by-default loading, 6-month filtering, empty/error/retry states, and clear state reset behavior.
Designed async request control for shared workspace loading, including cache validation, in-flight request reuse, and retry handling to reduce redundant server requests.
Defined interface behavior for filterLatest and mapped it through controller and service layers into timestamp-based backend request parameters.

matched_projects.project_title: MV-18357 Forms Conditional Logic UX Tune

tailored_bullets.text: Delivered a Vue-based UX enhancement for a form builder’s conditional-logic workflow, enabling bidirectional navigation between form elements and logic rules to improve traceability and editing efficiency.
Translated approved product/UX requirements into detailed web interaction design and implementation across FormBuilder/FormTemplateBuilder, FormCanvas, FormConditionPanel, and FormConditionItem.

Designed state-driven UI coordination using shared element IDs and source-aware selection logic to support synchronized panel focus, form scrolling, persistent selection, and temporary highlight behavior.
Solved lazy-loading navigation issues by revealing hidden form pages from store-backed form metadata before scrolling to and highlighting target fields or pages.
Delivered web-side technical design and implementation for MNF-463, a self-service onboarding and homepage optimization project in the New Flow product. :contentReference[oaicite:30]{index=30}

Translated PM-defined requirements into structured web behavior covering DNS registration animation, onboarding redirect/tour logic, Get Started tiles, recent flows/templates, and terminology updates. :contentReference[oaicite:31]{index=31}

Defined and implemented layered behavior across view, store/model, and controller, including state enums, fetch/reset actions, derived flags, compact list rendering, and workflow/template retrieval integration. :contentReference[oaicite:32]{index=32}

Implemented detailed interaction logic for first-time homepage onboarding, including route-based tour triggering, menu highlight sequencing, AI-entry routing, loading skeletons, and retry/error handling. :contentReference[oaicite:33]{index=33} :contentReference[oaicite:34]{index=34}

The Web Developer will work with a team to develop, maintain and enhance database-driven websites, applications and web elements for the public-facing websites of the University System of New Hampshire and its institutions with a strong focus toward user experience, digital accessibility, SEO principles and client specifications. The Web Developer provides technical leadership to support USNH strategic initiatives.  

In this role, you will develop and extend the Drupal content management system framework as well as develop software using PHP, JavaScript, jQuery, HTML, CSS, SQL, and/or other languages. Experience in Web Services (SOAP, REST) and source code control (GitLab) is required. 

You will also assist in establishing and maintaining a development environment that uses industry best practices for change management. This includes effective use of code repositories, properly leveraging development-test-production environments, and adherence to security protocols and auditable programming standards.

Duties/Responsibilities

Software Development (80%)

Develop websites and other software applications to meet client needs within the scope of the USNH institutions’ web style guides and policies. This includes designing, writing, and implementing new application software and enhancements to existing software; as well as designing application system solutions that employ current and emerging technologies.

Build standards-compliant websites using content management systems like Drupal and WordPress, and web technologies like PHP, HTML, React, and CSS.

Work with complex data structures within relational databases.

Collaborate across teams to integrate AI capabilities into workflows and align AI initiatives with organizational goals.

Leverage AI to improve code quality and automate coding tasks. This includes testing, debugging, and improving efficiency by analyzing code for vulnerabilities and suggesting improvements.

Troubleshoot issues as required.

Maintain technical skills to ensure currency with new and emerging web technologies.

Provide documentation and technical support for supported applications.

Coordinate the tracking, implementation, and testing of software.

Oversee the maintenance schedule for software applications to ensure they stay up-to-date with the latest stable versions and security releases.

Manage document libraries, Git and other information warehouses.

Client Services (15%)

Working closely with other project leaders, this position provides technical leadership by estimating project hours, developing functional and technical specifications, establishing timelines for software projects requested by our customers, and ensuring timely delivery of properly functioning application software.

Other (5%)

Other duties as assigned. May include training, supervision, professional development, and research.

Minimum Acceptable Education & Experience:
Bachelor’s degree and six years of experience in information technology field, Master’s and four years, or combination of education and experience equal to ten years.

Required Knowledge, Skills & Abilities:
Ability to be flexible, adapt and promote organizational core beliefs and values.

Demonstrated experience researching, analyzing, and implementing strategies to leverage technology to improve the user experience and/or develop new capabilities to meet emerging opportunities or challenges.

Experience in database-driven web applications, HTML5, CSS, JavaScript, React, PHP, Drupal or WordPress content management systems.

Working knowledge of at least one SQL-based database.

Knowledge of version control systems such as Git or SVN.

Strong organizational skills with effective oral and written communication skills.

Preferred Qualifications:

Relational database management experience.

Leadership experience.

Experience with project management tools and methodologies.

Experience working in higher education.

Customer service experience.

Applicant Instructions: 

Applicants should be prepared to upload the following documents when applying online within the My Experience: Resume/CV section of the application: (Maximum of 5 Documents)

Resume/CV

Cover Letter

Contact Information for 3 Professional References

Applications that are missing any of the required items may not move forward for consideration. Additional uploaded documents not requested in the position announcement will not be reviewed.

The University of New Hampshire is an R1 Carnegie classification research institution providing comprehensive, high-quality undergraduate and graduate programs of distinction. UNH is located in Durham on a 188-acre campus, 60 miles north of Boston and 8 miles from the Atlantic coast and is convenient to New Hampshire’s lakes and mountains. There is a student enrollment of 13,000 students, with a full-time faculty of over 600, offering 90 undergraduate and more than 70 graduate programs. The University actively promotes a dynamic learning environment in which qualified individuals of differing perspectives, life experiences, and cultural backgrounds pursue academic goals with mutual respect and shared inquiry.

EEO Statement

The University System of New Hampshire is an Equal Opportunity/Equal Access employer. The University System is committed to creating an environment that values and supports diversity and inclusiveness across our campus communities and encourages applications from qualified individuals who will help us achieve this mission. The University System prohibits discrimination on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, age, national origin, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, disability, genetic information, veteran status, or marital status.

Compensation Pay Range:

$69,560.00 - $124,580.00
The pay range for this position is listed above. Actual offer will be based on skills, qualifications, experience, and internal equity, in addition to relevant business considerations. More information on benefits can be found here: USNH Employee Benefits | Human Resources
